paper.title: 591 high velocity stars in the Galactic halo selected from LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2

extraction.summary: Extracted only the 20 fastest HiVelSCs that are tabulated in the paper with complete unbound probabilities. The full catalog of 591 candidates, including 43 unbound in the Watkins+2019 potential, is available only as an external CSV and could not be extracted from the provided PDF/LaTeX source. The 20 extracted stars are the fastest and have very high unbound probabilities.

method_chain.summary: LAMOST DR7 low-resolution spectroscopic survey providing spectra, radial velocities (1D pipeline) and atmospheric parameters (LASP).

source_refs.context: Description of LAMOST and LASP

method_chain.summary: Gaia DR2 astrometric parameters (positions, proper motions, parallaxes) and photometry (G, G_BP, G_RP).

source_refs.context: Gaia DR2 astrometry and photometry

method_chain.summary: Cross-match LAMOST DR7 with Gaia DR2 using TOPCAT with a 5 arcsec radius, producing the LAMOST-Gaia catalog of over 10 million entries.

source_refs.context: Cross-match using TOPCAT

method_chain.summary: Select 'low-f' samples: stars with positive parallax and fractional parallax error f_varpi <= 0.2. About 8.48 million entries pass.

source_refs.context: Selection of low-f samples

method_chain.summary: Adopt radial velocities from LASP, corrected by a zero-point of -5.4 km/s using the APOGEE radial velocity standard star catalog.

parameters.name: other
parameters.description: Zero-point correction applied to LASP RVs

source_refs.context: LASP RV zero-point correction

method_chain.summary: Adopt solar position and motion parameters: R0 = 8.2 kpc, z0 = 25 pc, local standard of rest circular velocity 238 km/s, solar peculiar motion (U, V, W) = (14.0, 12.24, 7.25) km/s.

source_refs.context: Sun-GC distance R0=8.2 kpc
Sun offset above disk z0=25 pc
LSR velocity 238 km/s
Solar peculiar motion U=14.0 km/s
Assumptions for distance and velocity calculation

method_chain.summary: Compute Galactocentric Cartesian positions (x,y,z), distances (r_GC), and velocities (Vx,Vy,Vz,V_GC) using Monte Carlo sampling (1000 realizations) of astrometry and corrected RVs, taking covariance into account. Use the solar motion and Galactic parameters from step-06.

source_refs.context: Monte Carlo method to compute r_GC and V_GC

method_chain.summary: Adopt seven Galactic potential models (Watkins+2019, Gnedin+2005, Kenyon+2014, Koposov+2010, Paczynski+1990, MWPotential2014, Xue+2008) to estimate escape velocities at each star's Galactocentric distance.

parameters.name: potential_name
parameters.raw_value: Watkins+2019, Gnedin+2005, Kenyon+2014, Koposov+2010, Paczynski+1990, MWPotential2014, Xue+2008
parameters.description: List of potential models used for escape velocities

source_refs.context: Seven potential models adopted

method_chain.summary: MWPotential2014 (from Galpy) used for orbit integration, consisting of a power-law bulge, Miyamoto-Nagai disk, and NFW dark-matter halo.

source_refs.context: Orbit integration uses Galpy MWPotential2014

method_chain.summary: Calculate escape velocities for each star under the seven potentials. Compute unbound probability P_ub as the fraction of 1000 MC realizations where V_GC > V_esc.

source_refs.context: Calculation of escape velocities and unbound probabilities

method_chain.summary: Perform orbit integration for each star back to 10 Gyr using Galpy with MWPotential2014 (bulge, disk, NFW halo). Compute orbit parameters: eccentricity e, maximum height Zmax, minimum crossing radius r_min, energy difference E-Phi(infinity).

source_refs.context: Orbit integration with Galpy MWPotential2014

method_chain.summary: Classify each candidate into HVS, HRS, RS, OUT using probabilities P_gc (fraction of orbits with r_min < 1 kpc), P_MW (r_min < 25 kpc), and P_ub (unbound). Criteria: HVS if P_gc > 0.16; HRS if P_gc < 0.16 and P_MW > 0.5 and P_ub > 0.5; RS if P_gc < 0.16, P_MW > 0.5, P_ub < 0.5; OUT if P_gc < 0.16, P_MW < 0.5.

source_refs.context: Origin classification criteria and table

method_chain.summary: Atmospheric parameters (Teff, log g, [Fe/H]) from LAMOST Stellar Parameter Pipeline (LASP).

source_refs.context: LASP atmospheric parameters

method_chain.summary: Additional stellar parameters from the DD-Payne method: effective temperature, surface gravity, metallicity, and [alpha/Fe] abundance.

source_refs.context: DD-Payne method for alpha abundance and atmospheric parameters

method_chain.summary: astrometric_flag: assign a flag (=1) if the star meets conservative astrometric quality criteria (C.1, C.2, last two criteria of selection A in Lindegren et al. 2018, and equality cuts from Marchetti et al. 2019).

source_refs.context: Definition of astrometric_flag

method_chain.summary: Spectral type classification from the LAMOST 1D pipeline, given as Class column.

source_refs.context: LASP provides spectral type

inclusion_assessment.summary: Hivel1 is the fastest HiVelSC with V_GC = 922 km/s, unbound probability P_ub(W)=0.996; classified as HRS candidate.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Tabulated unbound probabilities >0.5 under multiple potential models and explicit identification as possibly unbound.

source_refs.context: Discussion of unbound probabilities and model dependence
Abstract: report 591 high velocity star candidates from LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2

candidate_groups_considered.description: The full set of 591 HiVelSCs, available only as external CSV (resource-001), with only the 20 fastest tabulated in the paper. The remaining 571 candidates could not be extracted.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Only the 20 fastest HiVelSCs are tabulated in the paper with complete data; the external CSV containing all 591 candidates is not available for extraction.

source_refs.context: Main catalog URL and mention of 591 HiVelSCs
